dataList.dlg:
- We've received reports on everything you've gone through so far. I've read the observation logs as well.
- Everyone here's been eager to see what sort of people you might be. Individuals who prioritize action over theory are becoming a rarity these days, after all.

- Too many battle tests? Well, that's because—
- The reports suggested a high level of interest in these "Mirror Dungeons," so I designed the tests around similar experiences.
- That's like saying we like working overtime just because we do it all the time, Boss...

- Total damage rate has gone over the 70% mark.
- By LCE standards, that calls for aborting the test. How shall we proceed, Boss?

- Hmm... There should be no issues—their manager is with them. Continue the test.
- You heard him. The "abort" button is right there on the wall, though, should you find the battle too much to handle.

- Given the manager's usual command patterns, they're likely to zero in on the immobilized target more aggressively now.
- No—look at this section. The data says that they tend to shift their focus to another target when they have some leeway...
- Haha... Please don't mind us and focus on the test, LCB.

- It may be best to prioritize covering the incapacitated Sinners if you've no strategy in mind. - What if this is their strategy? - Well, it is what it is. Perhaps this will give us a post-test opportunity to document the manager's many expressions of pain.

- Entity Suppression complete. A bit different from the usual method listed in the manual, but not a bad outcome, don't you think?
- Let's make a note of the method and replicate it ourselves later.

- A Sinner has been killed.
- I do not doubt your capabilities, but do try not to grow desensitized. Although... considering the degree of pain stimuli involved, I doubt desensitization would come easily.

- Uh... Should we start over?
- No, let us proceed for the moment. Some data can only be observed and acquired under adverse conditions.

- The readings are looking solid. The test should wrap up smooth and clean per our prediction.
- Well, in light of the events they have weathered thus far, it would be quite anomalous indeed if they were to struggle with combat trial of this rudimentary level.

- This... is quite intriguing. The variables are decidedly unfavorable, but they seem to be approaching a solution nonetheless.
- So what do you think? Now you can tell what sort of principle the manager follows when leading the team, right?

- Boss. We should be moving on to the next item on the schedule, or we'll risk running out of time.
- Hmm. Already? The hour seems to have advanced beyond my accounting. Manager, it appears the time allotted to us grows rather scarce. Do proceed with expedition.

- A tepid response to such a threat would constitute a fruitless effort.
- Don't feel obligated to hold back from using E.G.O. The lab walls can take it.

- I hate Abnos and Distortions that do this.
- Yet, they're of the very same type that becomes much more manageable once enough data has been collected. Personally, I find them easier to handle than entities that respond only to overwhelming brute force.

- Looks like that entity won't be able to hold out for much longer.
- Naturally. After all, the manager has selected the most effective means of attack for this circumstance.

- What a creative use of that ability. Most fascinating.
- Boss, I'm pretty sure that was a mistake...
- I am aware. Fascination does not always equal commendation.

- If only new hires were as good at their jobs as they are.
- Wishful thinking. Abnormalities and Distortions both have so many managerial rules and resistances to memorize.

- Not that big of an error. Should we let it slide?
- Why not. Obviously, we cannot expect too much precision from ordinary minds.
- Uh-huh... Sure.

- The entity's state must have shifted somewhat. As soon as you catch it changing, tell us— I mean, write it down on paper.
- Don't forget to capture the manager's tick-tocking noises as well. We'll be analyzing their wavelengths and matching them to the situations in which they occur.

- Weakening the enemy is an acceptable tactic. Adequate work.
- "Adequate" coming from our boss? That's a huge compliment.

- You've been empowered due to external effects, but don't let that scare you.
- Try to think of potential avenues to exploit this opportunity.

- The Sinners' general ability parameters have plummeted.
- Probably not a great idea to let this effect linger too long.

- You really kicked that thing's hiney.
- The entity's structural integrity has dropped significantly.
- Did you identify that particular part as a potential source of risk? I would like to hear what went into your decision to remove it.

- Huh, didn't expect this battle to play out like this. They're suffering much more losses than anticipated.
- There must've been a command hiccup.

- How'd that happen? Arbitrary Identity selection? Unexpected mid-battle variables?
- Most likely a combination of many, as these failures often tend to be. I trust the manager will be more attentive in their next attempt.

- Extrapolated damage output is beyond all projections.
- Hm. I'm not sure if we even have any comparison indices for this data. Submit any similar test records to me posthaste.

- The extrapolated numbers exceed even Alyssa's when she raised her Attunement to 93%.
- Unlike the outliers extrapolated from the LCA and the LCD, these figures actually seem quite useful. This is good data.

- Nice moves. I wonder if they're borrowed from the Identity?
- Other possibilities include the Sinner's own prowess returning, or the manager's command having a positive impact on their capabilities.
- Let's put a pin in it for now and review it during analysis.

- So, how many wins does this make?
- 17 victories. I see—the chance of success rises significantly with the manager at the wheel.

- Could it be that this was too difficult for them to handle? Maybe we can lower the difficulty a little...
- Hm. No, no need. I'm sure this skirmish must've taught them some valuable lessons. Let's take a quick 5, then have them give it another go.

- The LCB Department will be arriving soon. All preparations are in order, I trust?
- Yes, Boss. Over 100 check-up processes, including 27 different combat tests, are ready and waiting.
- Haha... The workload would've been cut in half had we not procrastinated...
